page: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and cryptography extensions such as AES and SHA_NI. This instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 614 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark results position this instance primarily in the average tier for general compute and memory operations. The single-core stress-ng performance is recorded at 1900.18, while the multi-core score reaches 7763.87. Memory bandwidth is measured at 419.39 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 58.16 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Tradeoffs are evident in multi-threaded workloads; while single-threaded bzip3 decompression is average at 10.19 GB/sec, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falls into the weak category at 31.82 GB/sec. Similarly, LLM inference with a medium 7B model is weak, processing 17.28 tokens/sec for prompts and 7.5 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-8ds_v5 provides a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The inclusion of 614 GB of local storage alongside Premium Storage capability supports data-intensive applications. Factual benchmark indicators, such as a Redis SET throughput of 7.87 million operations per second and a Passmark database score of 4064.08, suggest this instance is appropriate for relational databases, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a Passmark CPU score of 11443.28 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.87 million operations per second. However, it exhibits performance trade-offs in highly parallel tasks, as shown by weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and low LLM inference speeds on 7B models. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core and local storage capacity, this instance is appropriate for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, suited for database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v5 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for data-intensive and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory density
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average database performance with 7.87 million Redis SET operations per second
